content.problemStatement:
You are given a 000-indexed binary string SSS of length N+1N + 1N+1, and your task is to construct an array AAA of length NNN such that:


SX=1S_X = 1SX‚Äã=1 (for 0‚â§X‚â§N0 \le X \le N0‚â§X‚â§N) if and only if there exists LLL and RRR such that:

1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N
MEX(A[L,R])=XMEX(A[L, R]) = XMEX(A[L,R])=X, where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã], and MEX denotes the minimum non-negative integer not present in the array.


You are guaranteed that:


For the given string SSS, there exists a valid array AAA, satisfying 0‚â§Ai‚â§N0 \le A_i \le N0‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§N.
S0=S1=1S_0 = S_1 = 1S0‚Äã=S1‚Äã=1, i.e. there exists at least one range in the array AAA with MEX of 000 and 111.
You are allowed to print any valid solution satisfying 0‚â§Ai‚â§N0 \le A_i \le N0‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string SSS of size N+1N + 1N+1.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, a valid solution. It is guaranteed one exists for all the input cases that are given to you.
constraints: The input is generated such that at least one valid array exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The array [0,2,0][0, 2, 0][0,2,0] has 666 subranges:

L=1,R=1L = 1, R = 1L=1,R=1 : [0][0][0] has a MEX of 111.
L=1,R=2L = 1, R = 2L=1,R=2 : [0,2][0, 2][0,2] has a MEX of 111.
L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 : [0,2,0][0, 2, 0][0,2,0] has a MEX of 111.
L=2,R=2L = 2, R = 2L=2,R=2 : [2][2][2] has a MEX of 000.
L=2,R=3L = 2, R = 3L=2,R=3 : [2,0][2, 0][2,0] has a MEX of 111.
L=3,R=3L = 3, R = 3L=3,R=3 : [0][0][0] has a MEX of 111.

Thus, the occurring MEX values are only 000 and 111, as required by our binary string.
